---
title: "Module 2 Assignment: Extracting Strategic Technology Signals from Form 10-K"
subtitle: "Building a Retrieval-Ready Corpus for LLM Systems"
number-sections: true
date: "2026-02-15"
date-modified: today
date-format: long
engine: jupyter
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: false
---

# Important Reminder {.unnumbered}

- This assignment may be completed using [Google Colab]{.uublue-bold} or [AWS Academy]{.uublue-bold}.
- You must reuse the [cleaned corpus created in Assignment 1]{.uugreen-bold}.
- The goal is to build a [retrieval-ready dataset for LLM systems]{.uured-bold}, not to train large models.
- Start with a small subset of filings and scale once your pipeline works.


# Submission Instructions {.unnumbered}

You must submit the following files on Blackboard:

## Word Document (`.docx`) {.unnumbered}

Maximum **5 pages** (excluding cover page and references).

The report should include:

- construct definition
- extraction strategy
- dataset summary
- short business interpretation

Do **not include code** in this document.

## Jupyter Notebook (`.ipynb`) {.unnumbered}

Your notebook must be:

- Fully executable
- Organized by Question (Q1–Q3)
- Clearly commented

Suggested structure:

1. Config and paths  
2. Load artifacts from Assignment 1  
3. Define extraction construct  
4. Candidate sentence extraction  
5. Chunk construction  
6. Embedding generation  
7. Dataset export  

## Generative AI Prompt Disclosure {.unnumbered}

If you use generative AI tools for any part of the assignment, you must disclose:
- which tools you used
- Each prompt and its outcome
- submit it as a word document

## Final Dataset {.unnumbered}

Submit the extracted dataset as either:

- `.csv`
- `.json`

This dataset will be used directly in **Assignment 3 for building a RAG system**.


# Objective {.unnumbered}

:::{.callout-note}
Assignment 1 focused on building a **general NLP corpus from corporate filings**.

Assignment 2 focuses on **targeted information extraction** and preparing a dataset that can support **LLM retrieval systems**.
:::

You will design a pipeline that extracts **one strategic technology signal** from corporate filings and converts it into a structured dataset suitable for **vector search and retrieval**.


# Business Context {.unnumbered}

Technology investors increasingly analyze corporate disclosures to understand how firms are investing in digital capabilities.

Important signals often appear in long narrative disclosures rather than structured financial tables.

Examples include references to:

- artificial intelligence investment
- cloud infrastructure expansion
- datacenter capacity
- technology platform upgrades
- digital transformation initiatives

These signals are buried in thousands of pages of filings.

Your task is to build a **pipeline that extracts and structures one such signal from Form 10-K filings**.


## SEC 2024 Form 10-K TXT Files {.unnumbered}

You will use the same **2024 SEC Form 10-K TXT filings** used in Assignment 1. All files are available in the shared folder:

[2024 SEC 10-K Filings](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1q7BfsNHCewG1zNfnqyCcBj9p_RUt-zW6?usp=sharing)
[2024 SEC 10-K Filings - HTML](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1tqqoqDfcOpGKBoRN7PpAtElcmzKcrbGz?usp=sharing)

You must reuse the **processed corpus created in Assignment 1**, including:

- cleaned section text
- sentence-level chunks
- company identifiers
- SIC codes

# Defining the Target Strategic Signal {#sec-q1}

Select **one technology-related signal** to extract from corporate filings.

Choose one of the following:

- Artificial Intelligence investment  
- Technology infrastructure spending  
- Datacenter / compute infrastructure expansion  
- Digital transformation initiatives  

Your goal is to convert a vague theme into a **clear extraction construct**.

## Technical Instructions

1. Define your signal clearly:
   - what language counts as evidence
   - what language does not count
2. Provide **example sentences** that represent valid evidence.
3. Identify **keywords or phrases** likely to appear in filings.

Examples may include terms related to:

- AI systems
- machine learning
- data infrastructure
- cloud computing
- computing capacity
- technology platforms

4. Decide which filing sections are most relevant, such as:

- Item 1 – Business  
- Item 1A – Risk Factors  
- Item 7 – Management Discussion & Analysis  

## Expected Outputs

- Definition of the signal construct
- Table of inclusion and exclusion examples
- Initial keyword list


# Extracting Evidence from the 10-K Corpus {#sec-q2}

Identify sentences or passages in filings that contain evidence of your selected signal.

## Technical Instructions

Using the cleaned corpus from Assignment 1:

1. Programmatically search filings using your keyword list.
2. Extract candidate sentences or paragraphs containing signal language.
3. Store extracted evidence along with metadata fields such as:

- company identifier
- SIC code
- filing section
- evidence text
- keyword trigger

4. Remove duplicates and obviously irrelevant matches.

## Example Output Schema

Your dataset should resemble the following structure:

```

company
sic
section
evidence_text
keyword_trigger

```

5. Provide a small sample of extracted evidence and manually verify that it reflects the intended signal.

## Expected Outputs

- extraction pipeline description
- table summarizing extracted records
- sample evidence sentences

---

# Building a Retrieval-Ready Dataset {#sec-q3}

Transform extracted evidence into a dataset suitable for **LLM retrieval systems**.

This dataset will later be used to build a **vector search system in Assignment 3**.

## Technical Instructions

1. Convert extracted evidence into **clean text chunks**.

2. Create a structured dataset with fields such as:

```

chunk_id
company
sic
section
signal_type
chunk_text

```

3. Generate **sentence embeddings** for each chunk using a Hugging Face model.

Examples include:

- sentence-transformers models
- other embedding models available in Python

4. Store embeddings alongside the metadata.

Example schema:

```

chunk_id
company
sic
section
signal_type
chunk_text
embedding_vector

```

5. Save the final dataset as `.csv` or `.json`.

## Expected Outputs

- summary statistics of the dataset
- number of companies represented
- number of evidence chunks extracted
- example embedding vectors

# Use of Generative AI {.unnumbered}

:::{.callout-important}
Generative AI tools may be used for:

- code assistance
- keyword discovery
- explanation
- summarization

However, you must still **build and validate the extraction pipeline yourself**.
:::

You must briefly document:

- where generative AI tools were used
- how outputs were validated or modified

---

# Looking Ahead {.unnumbered}

:::{.callout-note}

The dataset you create in this assignment will be reused in later modules.

Assignment 3 will introduce **vector retrieval systems**, where this dataset will become the **searchable corpus**.

Assignment 4 will extend the pipeline to **retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)** systems that answer questions using corporate disclosures.

:::

# Resources {.unnumbered}

- SEC EDGAR Filings Guide  
- Hugging Face Transformers Documentation  
- Sentence Transformers Documentation  
- PyTorch Documentation
